From: security.audit@tbs-sct.gc.ca
To: ngis-pm@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: itsec-director@ssc-spc.gc.ca; ciso@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: URGENT: ITSG-33 Compliance Gap — NGIS Phase 3 Network Migration
Date: Thu, 5 Mar 2026 08:47:15 -0500

Project Management Team,

During the scheduled ITSG-33 security compliance review for NGIS Phase 3
(review conducted February 28–March 4, 2026), our audit team identified
a Category 2 finding that must be addressed before the migration proceeds.

FINDING SUMMARY:
Control: IA-5 (Authenticator Management)
Status: NON-COMPLIANT
Detail: The proposed Active Directory migration plan does not include
documentation for privileged account management controls on the target
domain controllers. Specifically, the Security Assessment and
Authorization (SA&A) package is missing the Privileged Access Workstation
(PAW) architecture documentation required under ITSG-33 Annex 3A,
Control IA-5(1).

RISK: If the migration proceeds without resolving this finding, SSC's
PBMM cloud hosting authorization may be placed on hold, potentially
affecting other GC-Cloud workloads depending on the shared AD
infrastructure.

REQUIRED ACTIONS:
- The project team must provide the updated SA&A documentation addressing
  Control IA-5 to this office no later than March 12, 2026.
- A meeting between the SSC CISO office and the NGIS project team must
  be scheduled for the week of March 9 to review the PAW architecture.
- Until this finding is resolved, the March 18 migration window should
  be considered at risk.

Please confirm receipt and provide an action plan by end of day March 6.

Regards,
Office of the Chief Information Security Officer
Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat
security.audit@tbs-sct.gc.ca
